Title: Genome-Wide Identification of Direct Targets of ZjVND7 Reveals the Putative Roles of Whole-Genome Duplication in Sour Jujube in Regulating Xylem Vessel Differentiation and Drought Tolerance

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Most reliable binding motif for ZjVND7 is conserved between diploid and autotetraploid plants and contains the core sequence CTTNAAG; these motifs differ from previously reported Arabidopsis VND and maize NUT1 motifs, indicating divergence of the downstream regulatory network in sour jujube; the change in number and identity of ZjVND7 targets between diploids and autotetraploids may be influenced by epigenetic modifications retained in the genomic DNA used for DAP-seq.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq of diploid and autotetraploid sour jujube leaves under drought to define ZjVND7 binding peaks and motifs; reanalysis of previously generated RNA-seq data from diploid and autotetraploid leaves under control and drought conditions to assess expression of ZjVND7-targeted pathways.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NAC transcription factor whose direct DNA-binding targets are enriched in genes for xylem development, secondary wall synthesis, cell death, cell division, DNA replication and stress/hormone responses; shows stronger binding and a larger repertoire of promoter targets in autotetraploid sour jujube under drought, consistent with increased xylem vessel and parenchyma cell numbers and enhanced drought response in autotetraploids.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: ZjVND7 is a NAC transcription factor that binds preferentially to proximal promoter regions (within 2.0 kb upstream of TSS) with conserved CTTNAAG-containing motifs; binds a 19-nt motif including TTGCTT/AAGCAA; directly binds the ZjSMR1 promoter in yeast one-hybrid; in autotetraploids, shows increased number and strength of binding peaks, including to an ABRE (ACGTG) motif in the ZjSMR1 promoter, indicating transcriptional regulation of cell-cycle and stress-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in a xylem vessel differentiation and secondary cell wall regulatory network; controls downstream genes enriched in metabolic process, developmental process, response to stimulus, response to hormone, ABA signaling, cell death, cell division, and DNA replication; in autotetraploids targets additional pathways, including defense response, hydrogen peroxide metabolism, stress-related signal transduction, triterpenoid metabolism, flavonoid biosynthesis, photosynthesis, and glutathione metabolism under drought.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ZjVND7 -->(activation)--> ZjSMR1 (direct binding to ZjSMR1 promoter demonstrated by DAP-seq and yeast one-hybrid, with correlated expression changes under drought and ABA).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; secondary wall NAC (SWN) / VND subfamily
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, as shown by ZjVND7-eGFP fluorescence in transiently transformed tobacco leaf epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in leaves; under drought (soil water content 4% for 7 days), ZjVND7 shows a larger fold-change in autotetraploid leaves than in diploid leaves; expression is downregulated shortly after ABA spray (2 h) and then increases by 12 h, indicating ABA responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Ziziphus jujuba 'Dongzao' reference genome (version not specified)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed by withholding water until soil water content reached 4% field capacity for 7 days; foliar ABA treatment at 10 mg/L with sampling at 2 h and 12 h; comparison between diploid and colchicine-derived autotetraploid sour jujube.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Polyploidy effects on transcription factor regulatory networks; xylem vessel differentiation; drought and ABA response in sour jujube; NAC/VND transcriptional control of cell cycle and stress-related pathways.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance); xylem vessel differentiation and leaf vascular development; regulation of cell division and endoreduplication in response to drought and ABA in polyploid plants.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In autotetraploids, the number of xylem vessels and parenchyma cells in leaf main veins is nearly twice that in diploids; DAP-seq identified 1,759 vs 2,538 ZjVND7 peaks in diploid vs autotetraploid genomes, mapping to 236 vs 321 promoter-associated target genes, respectively.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Target genes in autotetraploids are more frequently annotated to defense response, positive regulation of cell death, hydrogen peroxide metabolism, and immune response-activating signal transduction compared with diploids, indicating broader potential impact of ZjVND7 on stress signaling pathways after whole-genome duplication.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Two-year-old diploid and autotetraploid sour jujube plants and long-term tissue culture seedlings; leaves used for drought and ABA treatments, RNA extraction, DAP-seq DNA preparation, and expression analysis; Nicotiana tabacum leaves used for transient expression of ZjVND7-eGFP; yeast strain EGY48 for yeast one-hybrid assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Ziziphus jujuba var. spinosa (sour jujube rootstock)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Histological analysis of leaf veins; RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis; gene cloning and sequencing; phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR using ZjActin reference and 2^-ΔΔCt quantification; DAP-seq library preparation, Illumina sequencing, read mapping (Bowtie2), peak calling (MACS2), promoter assignment (Homer), motif discovery (MEME-ChIP); GO and KEGG enrichment (KOBAS); reanalysis of published RNA-seq; subcellular localization using eGFP fusion in tobacco leaves; yeast one-hybrid using pB42AD and pLacZi2μ constructs in EGY48.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and phylogenetic analysis; qRT-PCR expression analysis in diploid and autotetraploid leaves under drought and ABA treatments; DAP-seq to map genome-wide binding peaks and motifs in diploid and autotetraploid genomes; GO/KEGG enrichment of bound targets; subcellular localization of ZjVND7-eGFP in tobacco leaves; yeast one-hybrid showing binding of ZjVND7 to the ZjSMR1 promoter.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length CDS identified as LOC107430472 from the Z. jujuba genome and cloned by RT-PCR from leaf cDNA, followed by sequencing and phylogenetic analysis to confirm orthology to Arabidopsis VND7/SWN NACs.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: ZjVND7 represents a key regulatory gene whose target network in autotetraploid sour jujube is enriched for xylem development and stress-response genes, and its altered binding and expression under drought provide a mechanistic entry point to modulate vessel number, parenchyma cell proliferation, and drought tolerance; understanding and manipulating ZjVND7 or its promoter binding sites may aid in breeding sour jujube rootstocks or polyploid lines with improved hydraulic efficiency and drought resilience.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ZjSMR1 was detected as a ZjVND7 target in one duplication in diploids and two duplications in autotetraploids, reflecting polyploidy-associated changes in promoter binding; analysis of ZjSMR1 promoter cis-elements identified a ZjVND7-targeted ABRE (ACGTG) motif specifically in autotetraploids, linking ZjSMR1 regulation to ABA signaling.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq of diploid and autotetraploid sour jujube under drought identified ZjSMR1 as a ZjVND7-bound target gene; reanalysis of existing RNA-seq datasets from diploid and autotetraploid leaves under control and drought conditions used to place ZjSMR1 within cell division and DNA endoreduplication pathways regulated by ZjVND7.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downstream cell division–related gene directly targeted by ZjVND7; carries DAP-seq peaks in both diploid and autotetraploid genomes with stronger binding signal in autotetraploids; expression is downregulated under drought and shows ABA-responsive dynamics similar to ZjVND7, supporting a role in cell division and DNA endoreduplication adjustments associated with increased xylem parenchyma cells and vessel differentiation, particularly in autotetraploids.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Promoter carries a ZjVND7-targeted ABRE (ACGTG) motif and is directly bound by ZjVND7 as shown by DAP-seq and yeast one-hybrid; stronger DAP-seq peak scores in autotetraploids (53 and 63) than in diploids (20) indicate enhanced binding; literature and discussion indicate SMR genes suppress cell cycle progression and promote endoreduplication, suggesting ZjSMR1 acts as a cell-cycle checkpoint regulator downstream of ZjVND7 in drought/ABA responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in a ZjVND7-centered regulatory pathway controlling cell division and DNA endoreduplication in xylem and leaf tissues under drought and ABA; implicated in the balance between cell division and differentiation of xylem parenchyma cells that precede vessel formation, and in stress-induced adjustments of the cell cycle/endoreduplication program.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: ZjVND7 -->(activation)--> ZjSMR1 (ZjVND7 binds the ZjSMR1 promoter in vitro and in yeast; ZjSMR1 expression changes mirror those of ZjVND7 under drought and ABA).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific SMR (SIAMESE-RELATED) cell cycle inhibitor family
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, based on ZjSMR1-eGFP fluorescence in transiently transformed tobacco leaf epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: ZjSMR1 expression is downregulated under drought stress in both diploid and autotetraploid sour jujube, with expression changes paralleling those of ZjVND7; after ABA spray on diploid plants, ZjSMR1 expression decreases markedly at 2 h and increases by 12 h, indicating ABA responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Ziziphus jujuba 'Dongzao' reference genome (version not specified) used for assigning DAP-seq peaks to the ZjSMR1 locus.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (4% soil water content for 7 days) applied to diploid and autotetraploid sour jujube; ABA foliar spray (10 mg/L) on diploid sour jujube with sampling at 2 h and 12 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Downstream effector of NAC/VND transcriptional network in sour jujube; regulation of cell division and endoreduplication under drought and ABA; contribution to xylem vessel differentiation differences between diploid and autotetraploid plants.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance); cell cycle and endoreduplication control; xylem parenchyma and vessel development in response to drought and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: DAP-seq peak score for ZjSMR1 in diploids is 20, while two peaks with scores 53 and 63 are detected in autotetraploids, indicating stronger binding of ZjVND7 to the ZjSMR1 promoter in autotetraploids.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Annotated to cell division and DNA endoreduplication pathways among ZjVND7 targets; proposed to contribute to the formation of more xylem parenchyma cells, which precede the increased number of vessel cells in autotetraploid veins compared with diploids.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Two-year-old diploid and autotetraploid sour jujube plants and tissue culture seedlings for drought treatments and RNA extraction; diploid sour jujube for ABA spray experiment; Nicotiana tabacum leaves for ZjSMR1-eGFP subcellular localization; yeast strain EGY48 for yeast one-hybrid with ZjVND7 and ZjSMR1 promoter constructs.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Ziziphus jujuba var. spinosa (sour jujube rootstock)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: DAP-seq peak calling and annotation to identify ZjVND7-bound genes annotated to cell division and DNA endoreduplication, including ZjSMR1; PCR amplification and cloning of a 474 bp ZjSMR1 promoter fragment into pLacZi2μ; yeast one-hybrid with ZjVND7 CDS cloned into pB42AD in EGY48; subcellular localization of ZjSMR1-eGFP in tobacco leaf cells; qRT-PCR to determine ZjSMR1 expression under drought and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq identification of ZjSMR1 promoter peaks bound by ZjVND7 in diploid and autotetraploid genomes; yeast one-hybrid confirming direct binding of ZjVND7 to a 474 bp ZjSMR1 promoter fragment; subcellular localization of ZjSMR1-eGFP in tobacco leaves; qRT-PCR measuring ZjSMR1 expression in diploid and autotetraploid plants under drought and in diploid plants after ABA treatment.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from DAP-seq data as a ZjVND7-bound gene annotated to cell division and DNA endoreduplication; promoter region (474 bp upstream fragment) amplified by PCR and cloned for yeast one-hybrid assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: ZjSMR1, as a directly ZjVND7-regulated cell-cycle control gene responsive to drought and ABA and with stronger promoter binding in autotetraploids, is a potential target to fine-tune the balance between cell division and endoreduplication in xylem parenchyma, thereby modulating vessel number and drought tolerance in sour jujube breeding, especially in autotetraploid rootstock lines.
